title: Tennis Star Carlos Alcaraz Will Miss the French Open Because of a Wrist Injury
summary:
Tennis fans, get ready for some sad news. Carlos Alcaraz, the two-time French Open champion, won't be playing in this year's tournament. He hurt his right wrist during a match in Barcelona last week.

At first, Alcaraz hoped it wasn't serious. But after getting tests done, he and his doctors decided he needs to rest. "We have decided that the most prudent (careful) thing to do is to be cautious and not participate in Rome or Roland Garros (the French Open)," Alcaraz said on social media.

This is a huge deal because Alcaraz is amazing on clay courts. He won the French Open in 2024 and 2025. In January, he became the youngest man ever to win all four major tennis tournaments (that's called a career Grand Slam) when he won the Australian Open. He's ranked number 2 in the world.

His big rival, Jannik Sinner from Italy, said he was sad to hear the news. "It's sad news for all of us. You want to play against the best players in the world, and he's definitely the best player on this surface," Sinner said. He hopes Alcaraz will be back for Wimbledon in July.

Alcaraz said he's not afraid to miss tournaments if it means getting better. "I'd rather come back maybe a bit later, but in great shape, than come back quickly and risk making this injury worse," he explained.

So the French Open, which runs from May 24 to June 7, will have to go on without one of its biggest stars. Get well soon, Carlos!
why_it_matters: This story shows you that even the best athletes have to take care of their bodies and sometimes miss important events to stay healthy — a lesson you can use in your own life, whether in sports, school, or any activity.

background_read: The French Open, also called Roland Garros, is one of the four most important tennis tournaments in the world, known as Grand Slams. It is played on clay courts, which make the ball bounce slower.
Carlos Alcaraz is from Spain and is famous for being very strong on clay. He became the youngest man ever to win all four Grand Slam tournaments when he won the Australian Open in January.
Injuries are common in tennis because players move quickly and put stress on their bodies. Many top players have missed tournaments to recover properly.
Article_Structure: WHO: Carlos Alcaraz (Spanish tennis star, two-time French Open champion).
WHAT: Alcaraz will miss the French Open due to a right wrist injury.
WHERE: The injury happened during a match in Barcelona; he will also miss the Italian Open in Rome.
WHY: He and his doctors decided it was prudent to rest and not risk making the injury worse.

perspectives.description: It's disappointing for tennis fans who were excited to see Alcaraz compete for a third French Open title. He is one of the most exciting players to watch.
Alcaraz is making a smart decision by resting now instead of risking a long-term injury. This shows maturity and a focus on his long career.
Alcaraz's absence opens the tournament for other players like Jannik Sinner, who said he wants to play against the best but also understands the need to heal.
